{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11491385]] Wikidata name: Kaiten-yama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11491385]] Wikidata name: Kaiten-yama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Marugame, Kagawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kaitenyama Kofun | native_name =快天山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kaiten-yama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kaitenyama Kofun, posterior circular portion to the right | map_type = Japan Kagawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kaitenyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.23888889|N|133.89055556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Marugame, Kagawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Shikoku]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|14|20|N|133|53|26|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kaitenyama Kofun'''|快天山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Ayauta, Kagawa|Ayauta]] neighborhood of the city of [[Marugame, Kagawa|Marugame]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], on the island of [[Shikoku]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2004.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140397|title=大野原古墳群|trans-title= 快天山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture and the third largest in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middleof the 4th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Kaitenyama Kofun is located at the tip of a ridge that emerges southward from the foot of Mt. Yokoyama in the central part of Kagawa Prefecture. On the top of the round part of the mound are the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] tombs of Kaiten, Kaizan, and Yuga, monks of the nearby Buddhist temple of Enpuku-ji, and the tumulus name of 'Kaitenzan' is derived from this. An exposed sarcophagus was discovered by a local junior high school student in 1950, which resulted in an [[archaeological excavation]], and several subsequent excavations have been carried out since then. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 98.8 meters, and was constructed in three or more tiers in the round posterior portion and three tiers in the rectangular anterior portion. The tumulus was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' lined the stops between tiers. No traces of a moat have been detected.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The anterior part of the mound has been partially leveled due to the construction of roads and poultry farms accompanying the development of farmland.
Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and one clay coffin burial facility were found, all within the round posterior portion, and all contain hollowed split bamboo-shaped sarcophaguses. This style of sarcophagus is centered on the Sanuki region of the early Kofun period, and th examples at this tumulus are the oldest known. The first and second chambers are arranged symmetrically from east to west across the center of the posterior circular part, and are lined with [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]]; whereas the third chamber is arranged to the north of the center point: however, all chambers are parallel to the main axis of the tumulus. Records indicate that an additional four more burials were found in the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus. but these were destroyed and the whereabouts of any artifacts recovered is unknown.
Numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were recovered from each surviving burial chamber, including [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, iron swords, iron axes, iron tools and farm implements, cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]''. These artifacts date the tumulus to the middleof the 4th century. Based on the remaining bones, the deceased in the second chamber was female, estimated to be 30–35 years old and that of the third chamber was male, estimated to be 27–28 years old.
The tumulus is about ten minutes on foot from [[:en:Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad]] [[:en:Kotohira Line|Kotohira Line]] [[:en:Kurikuma Station|Kurikuma Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 98.8 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 63.5 to 68 meter diameter x 10.55 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 4.33 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 32.5 meters

The {{nihongo|'''Kaitenyama Kofun'''|快天山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Ayauta, Kagawa|Ayauta]] neighborhood of the city of [[Marugame, Kagawa|Marugame]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], on the island of [[Shikoku]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2004.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140397|title=大野原古墳群|trans-title= 快天山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture and the third largest in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middleof the 4th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Kaitenyama Kofun is located at the tip of a ridge that emerges southward from the foot of Mt. Yokoyama in the central part of Kagawa Prefecture. On the top of the round part of the mound are the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] tombs of Kaiten, Kaizan, and Yuga, monks of the nearby Buddhist temple of Enpuku-ji, and the tumulus name of 'Kaitenzan' is derived from this. An exposed sarcophagus was discovered by a local junior high school student in 1950, which resulted in an [[archaeological excavation]], and several subsequent excavations have been carried out since then. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 98.8 meters, and was constructed in three or more tiers in the round posterior portion and three tiers in the rectangular anterior portion. The tumulus was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' lined the stops between tiers. No traces of a moat have been detected.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The anterior part of the mound has been partially leveled due to the construction of roads and poultry farms accompanying the development of farmland.
Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and one clay coffin burial facility were found, all within the round posterior portion, and all contain hollowed split bamboo-shaped sarcophaguses. This style of sarcophagus is centered on the Sanuki region of the early Kofun period, and th examples at this tumulus are the oldest known. The first and second chambers are arranged symmetrically from east to west across the center of the posterior circular part, and are lined with [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]]; whereas the third chamber is arranged to the north of the center point: however, all chambers are parallel to the main axis of the tumulus. Records indicate that an additional four more burials were found in the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus. but these were destroyed and the whereabouts of any artifacts recovered is unknown.
Numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were recovered from each surviving burial chamber, including [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, iron swords, iron axes, iron tools and farm implements, cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]''. These artifacts date the tumulus to the middleof the 4th century. Based on the remaining bones, the deceased in the second chamber was female, estimated to be 30–35 years old and that of the third chamber was male, estimated to be 27–28 years old.
The tumulus is about ten minutes on foot from [[:en:Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad]] [[:en:Kotohira Line|Kotohira Line]] [[:en:Kurikuma Station|Kurikuma Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 98.8 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 63.5 to 68 meter diameter x 10.55 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 4.33 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 32.5 meters
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kaiten-yama Kofun]][[en:Kaitenyama Kofun]][[ja:快天山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11491385]] Wikidata name: Kaiten-yama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11491385]] Wikidata name: Kaiten-yama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Marugame, Kagawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kaitenyama Kofun | native_name =快天山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kaiten-yama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kaitenyama Kofun, posterior circular portion to the right | map_type = Japan Kagawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kaitenyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.23888889|N|133.89055556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Marugame, Kagawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Shikoku]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|14|20|N|133|53|26|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kaitenyama Kofun'''|快天山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Ayauta, Kagawa|Ayauta]] neighborhood of the city of [[Marugame, Kagawa|Marugame]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], on the island of [[Shikoku]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2004.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140397|title=大野原古墳群|trans-title= 快天山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture and the third largest in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middleof the 4th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Kaitenyama Kofun is located at the tip of a ridge that emerges southward from the foot of Mt. Yokoyama in the central part of Kagawa Prefecture. On the top of the round part of the mound are the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] tombs of Kaiten, Kaizan, and Yuga, monks of the nearby Buddhist temple of Enpuku-ji, and the tumulus name of 'Kaitenzan' is derived from this. An exposed sarcophagus was discovered by a local junior high school student in 1950, which resulted in an [[archaeological excavation]], and several subsequent excavations have been carried out since then. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 98.8 meters, and was constructed in three or more tiers in the round posterior portion and three tiers in the rectangular anterior portion. The tumulus was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' lined the stops between tiers. No traces of a moat have been detected.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The anterior part of the mound has been partially leveled due to the construction of roads and poultry farms accompanying the development of farmland.
Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and one clay coffin burial facility were found, all within the round posterior portion, and all contain hollowed split bamboo-shaped sarcophaguses. This style of sarcophagus is centered on the Sanuki region of the early Kofun period, and th examples at this tumulus are the oldest known. The first and second chambers are arranged symmetrically from east to west across the center of the posterior circular part, and are lined with [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]]; whereas the third chamber is arranged to the north of the center point: however, all chambers are parallel to the main axis of the tumulus. Records indicate that an additional four more burials were found in the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus. but these were destroyed and the whereabouts of any artifacts recovered is unknown.
Numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were recovered from each surviving burial chamber, including [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, iron swords, iron axes, iron tools and farm implements, cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]''. These artifacts date the tumulus to the middleof the 4th century. Based on the remaining bones, the deceased in the second chamber was female, estimated to be 30–35 years old and that of the third chamber was male, estimated to be 27–28 years old.
The tumulus is about ten minutes on foot from [[:en:Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad]] [[:en:Kotohira Line|Kotohira Line]] [[:en:Kurikuma Station|Kurikuma Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 98.8 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 63.5 to 68 meter diameter x 10.55 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 4.33 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 32.5 meters
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Kaitenyama Kofun'''|快天山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Ayauta, Kagawa|Ayauta]] neighborhood of the city of [[Marugame, Kagawa|Marugame]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], on the island of [[Shikoku]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2004.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140397|title=大野原古墳群|trans-title= 快天山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture and the third largest in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middleof the 4th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Kaitenyama Kofun is located at the tip of a ridge that emerges southward from the foot of Mt. Yokoyama in the central part of Kagawa Prefecture. On the top of the round part of the mound are the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] tombs of Kaiten, Kaizan, and Yuga, monks of the nearby Buddhist temple of Enpuku-ji, and the tumulus name of 'Kaitenzan' is derived from this. An exposed sarcophagus was discovered by a local junior high school student in 1950, which resulted in an [[archaeological excavation]], and several subsequent excavations have been carried out since then. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 98.8 meters, and was constructed in three or more tiers in the round posterior portion and three tiers in the rectangular anterior portion. The tumulus was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' lined the stops between tiers. No traces of a moat have been detected.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The anterior part of the mound has been partially leveled due to the construction of roads and poultry farms accompanying the development of farmland.
Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and one clay coffin burial facility were found, all within the round posterior portion, and all contain hollowed split bamboo-shaped sarcophaguses. This style of sarcophagus is centered on the Sanuki region of the early Kofun period, and th examples at this tumulus are the oldest known. The first and second chambers are arranged symmetrically from east to west across the center of the posterior circular part, and are lined with [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]]; whereas the third chamber is arranged to the north of the center point: however, all chambers are parallel to the main axis of the tumulus. Records indicate that an additional four more burials were found in the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus. but these were destroyed and the whereabouts of any artifacts recovered is unknown.
Numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were recovered from each surviving burial chamber, including [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, iron swords, iron axes, iron tools and farm implements, cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]''. These artifacts date the tumulus to the middleof the 4th century. Based on the remaining bones, the deceased in the second chamber was female, estimated to be 30–35 years old and that of the third chamber was male, estimated to be 27–28 years old.
The tumulus is about ten minutes on foot from [[:en:Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad]] [[:en:Kotohira Line|Kotohira Line]] [[:en:Kurikuma Station|Kurikuma Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 98.8 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 63.5 to 68 meter diameter x 10.55 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 4.33 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 32.5 meters

The {{nihongo|'''Kaitenyama Kofun'''|快天山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Ayauta, Kagawa|Ayauta]] neighborhood of the city of [[Marugame, Kagawa|Marugame]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], on the island of [[Shikoku]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2004.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140397|title=大野原古墳群|trans-title= 快天山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture and the third largest in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middleof the 4th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Kaitenyama Kofun is located at the tip of a ridge that emerges southward from the foot of Mt. Yokoyama in the central part of Kagawa Prefecture. On the top of the round part of the mound are the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] tombs of Kaiten, Kaizan, and Yuga, monks of the nearby Buddhist temple of Enpuku-ji, and the tumulus name of 'Kaitenzan' is derived from this. An exposed sarcophagus was discovered by a local junior high school student in 1950, which resulted in an [[archaeological excavation]], and several subsequent excavations have been carried out since then. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 98.8 meters, and was constructed in three or more tiers in the round posterior portion and three tiers in the rectangular anterior portion. The tumulus was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' lined the stops between tiers. No traces of a moat have been detected.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The anterior part of the mound has been partially leveled due to the construction of roads and poultry farms accompanying the development of farmland.
Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and one clay coffin burial facility were found, all within the round posterior portion, and all contain hollowed split bamboo-shaped sarcophaguses. This style of sarcophagus is centered on the Sanuki region of the early Kofun period, and th examples at this tumulus are the oldest known. The first and second chambers are arranged symmetrically from east to west across the center of the posterior circular part, and are lined with [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]]; whereas the third chamber is arranged to the north of the center point: however, all chambers are parallel to the main axis of the tumulus. Records indicate that an additional four more burials were found in the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus. but these were destroyed and the whereabouts of any artifacts recovered is unknown.
Numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were recovered from each surviving burial chamber, including [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, iron swords, iron axes, iron tools and farm implements, cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]''. These artifacts date the tumulus to the middleof the 4th century. Based on the remaining bones, the deceased in the second chamber was female, estimated to be 30–35 years old and that of the third chamber was male, estimated to be 27–28 years old.
The tumulus is about ten minutes on foot from [[:en:Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad]] [[:en:Kotohira Line|Kotohira Line]] [[:en:Kurikuma Station|Kurikuma Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 98.8 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 63.5 to 68 meter diameter x 10.55 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 4.33 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 32.5 meters

The tumulus was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' lined the stops between tiers. No traces of a moat have been detected.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The anterior part of the mound has been partially leveled due to the construction of roads and poultry farms accompanying the development of farmland.
Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and one clay coffin burial facility were found, all within the round posterior portion, and all contain hollowed split bamboo-shaped sarcophaguses. This style of sarcophagus is centered on the Sanuki region of the early Kofun period, and th examples at this tumulus are the oldest known. The first and second chambers are arranged symmetrically from east to west across the center of the posterior circular part, and are lined with [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]]; whereas the third chamber is arranged to the north of the center point: however, all chambers are parallel to the main axis of the tumulus. Records indicate that an additional four more burials were found in the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus. but these were destroyed and the whereabouts of any artifacts recovered is unknown.
Numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were recovered from each surviving burial chamber, including [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, iron swords, iron axes, iron tools and farm implements, cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]''. These artifacts date the tumulus to the middleof the 4th century. Based on the remaining bones, the deceased in the second chamber was female, estimated to be 30–35 years old and that of the third chamber was male, estimated to be 27–28 years old.
The tumulus is about ten minutes on foot from [[:en:Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad]] [[:en:Kotohira Line|Kotohira Line]] [[:en:Kurikuma Station|Kurikuma Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kaitenyama Kofun'''|快天山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Ayauta, Kagawa|Ayauta]] neighborhood of the city of [[Marugame, Kagawa|Marugame]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], on the island of [[Shikoku]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2004.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140397|title=大野原古墳群|trans-title= 快天山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture and the third largest in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middleof the 4th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Kaitenyama Kofun is located at the tip of a ridge that emerges southward from the foot of Mt. Yokoyama in the central part of Kagawa Prefecture. On the top of the round part of the mound are the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] tombs of Kaiten, Kaizan, and Yuga, monks of the nearby Buddhist temple of Enpuku-ji, and the tumulus name of 'Kaitenzan' is derived from this. An exposed sarcophagus was discovered by a local junior high school student in 1950, which resulted in an [[archaeological excavation]], and several subsequent excavations have been carried out since then. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 98.8 meters, and was constructed in three or more tiers in the round posterior portion and three tiers in the rectangular anterior portion. The tumulus was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' lined the stops between tiers. No traces of a moat have been detected.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The anterior part of the mound has been partially leveled due to the construction of roads and poultry farms accompanying the development of farmland.
Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and one clay coffin burial facility were found, all within the round posterior portion, and all contain hollowed split bamboo-shaped sarcophaguses. This style of sarcophagus is centered on the Sanuki region of the early Kofun period, and th examples at this tumulus are the oldest known. The first and second chambers are arranged symmetrically from east to west across the center of the posterior circular part, and are lined with [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]]; whereas the third chamber is arranged to the north of the center point: however, all chambers are parallel to the main axis of the tumulus. Records indicate that an additional four more burials were found in the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus. but these were destroyed and the whereabouts of any artifacts recovered is unknown.
Numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were recovered from each surviving burial chamber, including [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, iron swords, iron axes, iron tools and farm implements, cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]''. These artifacts date the tumulus to the middleof the 4th century. Based on the remaining bones, the deceased in the second chamber was female, estimated to be 30–35 years old and that of the third chamber was male, estimated to be 27–28 years old.
The tumulus is about ten minutes on foot from [[:en:Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad]] [[:en:Kotohira Line|Kotohira Line]] [[:en:Kurikuma Station|Kurikuma Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 98.8 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 63.5 to 68 meter diameter x 10.55 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 4.33 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 32.5 meters

The {{nihongo|'''Kaitenyama Kofun'''|快天山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Ayauta, Kagawa|Ayauta]] neighborhood of the city of [[Marugame, Kagawa|Marugame]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], on the island of [[Shikoku]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2004.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140397|title=大野原古墳群|trans-title= 快天山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture and the third largest in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middleof the 4th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Kaitenyama Kofun is located at the tip of a ridge that emerges southward from the foot of Mt. Yokoyama in the central part of Kagawa Prefecture. On the top of the round part of the mound are the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] tombs of Kaiten, Kaizan, and Yuga, monks of the nearby Buddhist temple of Enpuku-ji, and the tumulus name of 'Kaitenzan' is derived from this. An exposed sarcophagus was discovered by a local junior high school student in 1950, which resulted in an [[archaeological excavation]], and several subsequent excavations have been carried out since then. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 98.8 meters, and was constructed in three or more tiers in the round posterior portion and three tiers in the rectangular anterior portion. The tumulus was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' lined the stops between tiers. No traces of a moat have been detected.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The anterior part of the mound has been partially leveled due to the construction of roads and poultry farms accompanying the development of farmland.
Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and one clay coffin burial facility were found, all within the round posterior portion, and all contain hollowed split bamboo-shaped sarcophaguses. This style of sarcophagus is centered on the Sanuki region of the early Kofun period, and th examples at this tumulus are the oldest known. The first and second chambers are arranged symmetrically from east to west across the center of the posterior circular part, and are lined with [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]]; whereas the third chamber is arranged to the north of the center point: however, all chambers are parallel to the main axis of the tumulus. Records indicate that an additional four more burials were found in the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus. but these were destroyed and the whereabouts of any artifacts recovered is unknown.
Numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were recovered from each surviving burial chamber, including [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, iron swords, iron axes, iron tools and farm implements, cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]''. These artifacts date the tumulus to the middleof the 4th century. Based on the remaining bones, the deceased in the second chamber was female, estimated to be 30–35 years old and that of the third chamber was male, estimated to be 27–28 years old.
The tumulus is about ten minutes on foot from [[:en:Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad]] [[:en:Kotohira Line|Kotohira Line]] [[:en:Kurikuma Station|Kurikuma Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 98.8 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 63.5 to 68 meter diameter x 10.55 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 4.33 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 32.5 meters

{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|14|20|N|133|53|26|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kaitenyama Kofun'''|快天山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Ayauta, Kagawa|Ayauta]] neighborhood of the city of [[Marugame, Kagawa|Marugame]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], on the island of [[Shikoku]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2004.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140397|title=大野原古墳群|trans-title= 快天山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture and the third largest in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middleof the 4th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Kaitenyama Kofun is located at the tip of a ridge that emerges southward from the foot of Mt. Yokoyama in the central part of Kagawa Prefecture. On the top of the round part of the mound are the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] tombs of Kaiten, Kaizan, and Yuga, monks of the nearby Buddhist temple of Enpuku-ji, and the tumulus name of 'Kaitenzan' is derived from this. An exposed sarcophagus was discovered by a local junior high school student in 1950, which resulted in an [[archaeological excavation]], and several subsequent excavations have been carried out since then. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 98.8 meters, and was constructed in three or more tiers in the round posterior portion and three tiers in the rectangular anterior portion. The tumulus was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' lined the stops between tiers. No traces of a moat have been detected.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The anterior part of the mound has been partially leveled due to the construction of roads and poultry farms accompanying the development of farmland.
Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and one clay coffin burial facility were found, all within the round posterior portion, and all contain hollowed split bamboo-shaped sarcophaguses. This style of sarcophagus is centered on the Sanuki region of the early Kofun period, and th examples at this tumulus are the oldest known. The first and second chambers are arranged symmetrically from east to west across the center of the posterior circular part, and are lined with [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]]; whereas the third chamber is arranged to the north of the center point: however, all chambers are parallel to the main axis of the tumulus. Records indicate that an additional four more burials were found in the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus. but these were destroyed and the whereabouts of any artifacts recovered is unknown.
Numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were recovered from each surviving burial chamber, including [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, iron swords, iron axes, iron tools and farm implements, cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]''. These artifacts date the tumulus to the middleof the 4th century. Based on the remaining bones, the deceased in the second chamber was female, estimated to be 30–35 years old and that of the third chamber was male, estimated to be 27–28 years old.
The tumulus is about ten minutes on foot from [[:en:Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad]] [[:en:Kotohira Line|Kotohira Line]] [[:en:Kurikuma Station|Kurikuma Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 98.8 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 63.5 to 68 meter diameter x 10.55 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 4.33 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 32.5 meters
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)]]

{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|14|20|N|133|53|26|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kaitenyama Kofun'''|快天山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the {{ill|Ayauta, Kagawa|en|Ayauta, Kagawa|WD=Q4830997|lt=Ayauta}} neighborhood of the city of [[Marugame, Kagawa|Marugame]], {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}, on the island of [[Shikoku]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2004.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140397|title=大野原古墳群|trans-title= 快天山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture and the third largest in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middleof the 4th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Kaitenyama Kofun is located at the tip of a ridge that emerges southward from the foot of Mt. Yokoyama in the central part of Kagawa Prefecture. On the top of the round part of the mound are the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] tombs of Kaiten, Kaizan, and Yuga, monks of the nearby Buddhist temple of Enpuku-ji, and the tumulus name of 'Kaitenzan' is derived from this. An exposed sarcophagus was discovered by a local junior high school student in 1950, which resulted in an [[archaeological excavation]], and several subsequent excavations have been carried out since then. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 98.8 meters, and was constructed in three or more tiers in the round posterior portion and three tiers in the rectangular anterior portion. The tumulus was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' lined the stops between tiers. No traces of a moat have been detected.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The anterior part of the mound has been partially leveled due to the construction of roads and poultry farms accompanying the development of farmland.
Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and one clay coffin burial facility were found, all within the round posterior portion, and all contain hollowed split bamboo-shaped sarcophaguses. This style of sarcophagus is centered on the Sanuki region of the early Kofun period, and th examples at this tumulus are the oldest known. The first and second chambers are arranged symmetrically from east to west across the center of the posterior circular part, and are lined with {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite}}; whereas the third chamber is arranged to the north of the center point: however, all chambers are parallel to the main axis of the tumulus. Records indicate that an additional four more burials were found in the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus. but these were destroyed and the whereabouts of any artifacts recovered is unknown.
Numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were recovered from each surviving burial chamber, including {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, iron swords, iron axes, iron tools and farm implements, cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]''. These artifacts date the tumulus to the middleof the 4th century. Based on the remaining bones, the deceased in the second chamber was female, estimated to be 30–35 years old and that of the third chamber was male, estimated to be 27–28 years old.
The tumulus is about ten minutes on foot from {{ill|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|en|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|WD=Q566998|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad}} [[:en:Kotohira Line|Kotohira Line]] {{ill|Kurikuma Station|en|Kurikuma Station|WD=Q11536023|lt=Kurikuma Station}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 98.8 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 63.5 to 68 meter diameter x 10.55 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 4.33 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 32.5 meters
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|WD=Q6573268|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Marugame, Kagawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kaitenyama Kofun | native_name =快天山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kaiten-yama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kaitenyama Kofun, posterior circular portion to the right | map_type = Japan Kagawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kaitenyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.23888889|N|133.89055556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Marugame, Kagawa]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Shikoku]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|14|20|N|133|53|26|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kaitenyama Kofun'''|快天山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the {{ill|Ayauta, Kagawa|en|Ayauta, Kagawa|WD=Q4830997|lt=Ayauta}} neighborhood of the city of [[Marugame, Kagawa|Marugame]], {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}, on the island of [[Shikoku]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2004.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140397|title=大野原古墳群|trans-title= 快天山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture and the third largest in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middleof the 4th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Kaitenyama Kofun is located at the tip of a ridge that emerges southward from the foot of Mt. Yokoyama in the central part of Kagawa Prefecture. On the top of the round part of the mound are the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] tombs of Kaiten, Kaizan, and Yuga, monks of the nearby Buddhist temple of Enpuku-ji, and the tumulus name of 'Kaitenzan' is derived from this. An exposed sarcophagus was discovered by a local junior high school student in 1950, which resulted in an [[archaeological excavation]], and several subsequent excavations have been carried out since then. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 98.8 meters, and was constructed in three or more tiers in the round posterior portion and three tiers in the rectangular anterior portion. The tumulus was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' lined the stops between tiers. No traces of a moat have been detected.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The anterior part of the mound has been partially leveled due to the construction of roads and poultry farms accompanying the development of farmland.
Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and one clay coffin burial facility were found, all within the round posterior portion, and all contain hollowed split bamboo-shaped sarcophaguses. This style of sarcophagus is centered on the Sanuki region of the early Kofun period, and th examples at this tumulus are the oldest known. The first and second chambers are arranged symmetrically from east to west across the center of the posterior circular part, and are lined with {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite}}; whereas the third chamber is arranged to the north of the center point: however, all chambers are parallel to the main axis of the tumulus. Records indicate that an additional four more burials were found in the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus. but these were destroyed and the whereabouts of any artifacts recovered is unknown.
Numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were recovered from each surviving burial chamber, including {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, iron swords, iron axes, iron tools and farm implements, cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]''. These artifacts date the tumulus to the middleof the 4th century. Based on the remaining bones, the deceased in the second chamber was female, estimated to be 30–35 years old and that of the third chamber was male, estimated to be 27–28 years old.
The tumulus is about ten minutes on foot from {{ill|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|en|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|WD=Q566998|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad}} [[:en:Kotohira Line|Kotohira Line]] {{ill|Kurikuma Station|en|Kurikuma Station|WD=Q11536023|lt=Kurikuma Station}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 98.8 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 63.5 to 68 meter diameter x 10.55 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 4.33 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 32.5 meters
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|WD=Q6573268|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links==
*[https://www.city.marugame.lg.jp/sightseeing/history/03/page_10.html Marugame City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Marugame, Kagawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kaitenyama Kofun | native_name =快天山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kaiten-yama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kaitenyama Kofun, posterior circular portion to the right | map_type = Japan Kagawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kaitenyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.23888889|N|133.89055556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Marugame, Kagawa]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Shikoku]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|14|20|N|133|53|26|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kaitenyama Kofun'''|快天山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the {{ill|Ayauta, Kagawa|en|Ayauta, Kagawa|WD=Q4830997|lt=Ayauta|1=Ayauta, Kagawa}} neighborhood of the city of [[Marugame, Kagawa|Marugame]], {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, on the island of [[Shikoku]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2004.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140397|title=大野原古墳群|trans-title= 快天山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture and the third largest in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middleof the 4th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Kaitenyama Kofun is located at the tip of a ridge that emerges southward from the foot of Mt. Yokoyama in the central part of Kagawa Prefecture. On the top of the round part of the mound are the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] tombs of Kaiten, Kaizan, and Yuga, monks of the nearby Buddhist temple of Enpuku-ji, and the tumulus name of 'Kaitenzan' is derived from this. An exposed sarcophagus was discovered by a local junior high school student in 1950, which resulted in an [[archaeological excavation]], and several subsequent excavations have been carried out since then. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 98.8 meters, and was constructed in three or more tiers in the round posterior portion and three tiers in the rectangular anterior portion. The tumulus was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' lined the stops between tiers. No traces of a moat have been detected.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The anterior part of the mound has been partially leveled due to the construction of roads and poultry farms accompanying the development of farmland.
Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and one clay coffin burial facility were found, all within the round posterior portion, and all contain hollowed split bamboo-shaped sarcophaguses. This style of sarcophagus is centered on the Sanuki region of the early Kofun period, and th examples at this tumulus are the oldest known. The first and second chambers are arranged symmetrically from east to west across the center of the posterior circular part, and are lined with {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}}; whereas the third chamber is arranged to the north of the center point: however, all chambers are parallel to the main axis of the tumulus. Records indicate that an additional four more burials were found in the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus. but these were destroyed and the whereabouts of any artifacts recovered is unknown.
Numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were recovered from each surviving burial chamber, including {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, iron swords, iron axes, iron tools and farm implements, cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]''. These artifacts date the tumulus to the middleof the 4th century. Based on the remaining bones, the deceased in the second chamber was female, estimated to be 30–35 years old and that of the third chamber was male, estimated to be 27–28 years old.
The tumulus is about ten minutes on foot from {{ill|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|en|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|WD=Q566998|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|1=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad}} [[:en:Kotohira Line|Kotohira Line]] {{ill|Kurikuma Station|en|Kurikuma Station|WD=Q11536023|lt=Kurikuma Station|1=Kurikuma Station}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 98.8 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 63.5 to 68 meter diameter x 10.55 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 4.33 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 32.5 meters
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|WD=Q6573268|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Marugame, Kagawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kaitenyama Kofun | native_name =快天山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kaiten-yama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kaitenyama Kofun, posterior circular portion to the right | map_type = Japan Kagawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kaitenyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.23888889|N|133.89055556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Marugame, Kagawa]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Shikoku]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|14|20|N|133|53|26|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kaitenyama Kofun'''|快天山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the {{ill|Ayauta, Kagawa|en|Ayauta, Kagawa|WD=Q4830997|lt=Ayauta|1=Ayauta, Kagawa}} neighborhood of the city of [[Marugame, Kagawa|Marugame]], {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, on the island of [[Shikoku]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2004.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140397|title=大野原古墳群|trans-title= 快天山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture and the third largest in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middleof the 4th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Kaitenyama Kofun is located at the tip of a ridge that emerges southward from the foot of Mt. Yokoyama in the central part of Kagawa Prefecture. On the top of the round part of the mound are the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] tombs of Kaiten, Kaizan, and Yuga, monks of the nearby Buddhist temple of Enpuku-ji, and the tumulus name of 'Kaitenzan' is derived from this. An exposed sarcophagus was discovered by a local junior high school student in 1950, which resulted in an [[archaeological excavation]], and several subsequent excavations have been carried out since then. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 98.8 meters, and was constructed in three or more tiers in the round posterior portion and three tiers in the rectangular anterior portion. The tumulus was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' lined the stops between tiers. No traces of a moat have been detected.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The anterior part of the mound has been partially leveled due to the construction of roads and poultry farms accompanying the development of farmland.
Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and one clay coffin burial facility were found, all within the round posterior portion, and all contain hollowed split bamboo-shaped sarcophaguses. This style of sarcophagus is centered on the Sanuki region of the early Kofun period, and th examples at this tumulus are the oldest known. The first and second chambers are arranged symmetrically from east to west across the center of the posterior circular part, and are lined with {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}}; whereas the third chamber is arranged to the north of the center point: however, all chambers are parallel to the main axis of the tumulus. Records indicate that an additional four more burials were found in the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus. but these were destroyed and the whereabouts of any artifacts recovered is unknown.
Numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were recovered from each surviving burial chamber, including {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, iron swords, iron axes, iron tools and farm implements, cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]''. These artifacts date the tumulus to the middleof the 4th century. Based on the remaining bones, the deceased in the second chamber was female, estimated to be 30–35 years old and that of the third chamber was male, estimated to be 27–28 years old.
The tumulus is about ten minutes on foot from {{ill|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|en|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|WD=Q566998|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|1=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad}} [[:en:Kotohira Line|Kotohira Line]] {{ill|Kurikuma Station|en|Kurikuma Station|WD=Q11536023|lt=Kurikuma Station|1=Kurikuma Station}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 98.8 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 63.5 to 68 meter diameter x 10.55 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 4.33 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 32.5 meters
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|WD=Q6573268|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links==
*[https://www.city.marugame.lg.jp/sightseeing/history/03/page_10.html Marugame City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Marugame, Kagawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kaitenyama Kofun | native_name =快天山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kaiten-yama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kaitenyama Kofun, posterior circular portion to the right | map_type = Japan Kagawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kaitenyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.23888889|N|133.89055556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Marugame, Kagawa]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Shikoku]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|14|20|N|133|53|26|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kaitenyama Kofun'''|快天山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the {{ill|Ayauta, Kagawa|en|Ayauta, Kagawa|qid=Q4830997|lt=Ayauta|1=Ayauta, Kagawa}} neighborhood of the city of [[Marugame, Kagawa|Marugame]], {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, on the island of [[Shikoku]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2004.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140397|title=大野原古墳群|trans-title= 快天山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture and the third largest in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middleof the 4th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Kaitenyama Kofun is located at the tip of a ridge that emerges southward from the foot of Mt. Yokoyama in the central part of Kagawa Prefecture. On the top of the round part of the mound are the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] tombs of Kaiten, Kaizan, and Yuga, monks of the nearby Buddhist temple of Enpuku-ji, and the tumulus name of 'Kaitenzan' is derived from this. An exposed sarcophagus was discovered by a local junior high school student in 1950, which resulted in an [[archaeological excavation]], and several subsequent excavations have been carried out since then. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 98.8 meters, and was constructed in three or more tiers in the round posterior portion and three tiers in the rectangular anterior portion. The tumulus was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' lined the stops between tiers. No traces of a moat have been detected.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The anterior part of the mound has been partially leveled due to the construction of roads and poultry farms accompanying the development of farmland.
Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and one clay coffin burial facility were found, all within the round posterior portion, and all contain hollowed split bamboo-shaped sarcophaguses. This style of sarcophagus is centered on the Sanuki region of the early Kofun period, and th examples at this tumulus are the oldest known. The first and second chambers are arranged symmetrically from east to west across the center of the posterior circular part, and are lined with {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|qid=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}}; whereas the third chamber is arranged to the north of the center point: however, all chambers are parallel to the main axis of the tumulus. Records indicate that an additional four more burials were found in the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus. but these were destroyed and the whereabouts of any artifacts recovered is unknown.
Numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were recovered from each surviving burial chamber, including {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, iron swords, iron axes, iron tools and farm implements, cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]''. These artifacts date the tumulus to the middleof the 4th century. Based on the remaining bones, the deceased in the second chamber was female, estimated to be 30–35 years old and that of the third chamber was male, estimated to be 27–28 years old.
The tumulus is about ten minutes on foot from {{ill|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|en|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|qid=Q566998|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|1=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad}} [[:en:Kotohira Line|Kotohira Line]] {{ill|Kurikuma Station|en|Kurikuma Station|qid=Q11536023|lt=Kurikuma Station|1=Kurikuma Station}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 98.8 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 63.5 to 68 meter diameter x 10.55 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 4.33 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 32.5 meters
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|qid=Q6573268|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links==
*[https://www.city.marugame.lg.jp/sightseeing/history/03/page_10.html Marugame City home page] {{in lang|ja}}
